title: jasminum azoricum var. fluminense
intro: Jasminum azoricum var. fluminense, also known as Brazilian night-blooming jasmine, is a species of flowering plant in the olive family (Oleaceae) that is native to Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Brazilian night-blooming jasmine include its ability to reduce stress, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, which can be used in herbal remedies, aromatherapy, and skincare products.
